content.content:
1	# The protlms Container Contract
2	
3	> **Contract version:** `0.3`
4	>
5	> This document is the agreement between the `protlms` client and every model
6	> image. It is the *only* thing the two sides share. Its schemas are mirrored
7	> exactly by the Pydantic models in [`src/protlms/contract.py`](../src/protlms/contract.py);
8	> **edit the two together.** The worked examples here are checked in under
9	> [`tests/data/`](../tests/data/) and validated by `tests/test_contract.py`, so a
10	> drift between this document and the code fails a test.
11	
12	A model image is a black box that exposes a small command-line interface. The
13	client never imports model code, never knows a model's tokenizer, and never
14	sees its weights. It resolves a model name to an image, runs the image with a
15	read-only input mount and a writable output mount, and parses the files the
16	image leaves behind.
17	
18	---
19	
20	## 1. Versioning & compatibility
21	
22	`contract_version` is a `MAJOR.MINOR` string. The client refuses to run against
23	an image whose **major** differs from its own.
24	
25	| Image vs. client | Behavior |
26	|---|---|
27	| Same major, image minor ≤ client minor | Run normally. |
28	| Same major, image minor > client minor | Run; the client may warn. Unknown manifest/result fields are ignored. |
29	| Different major | Refuse with `ContractVersionError`. |
30	
31	Forward compatibility relies on the client ignoring unknown fields
32	(`extra="ignore"` on every model). Adding a new capability or field is a
33	**minor** bump; changing or removing existing semantics is a **major** bump.
34	
35	---
36	
37	## 2. Internal CLI
38	
39	The image's `ENTRYPOINT` is the contract CLI. It MUST expose these subcommands:
40	
41	```
42	<entry> manifest
43	        # Print the manifest JSON (section 3) to stdout and exit 0.
44	        # Requires no mounts — introspectable with a one-shot `docker run`.
45	
46	<entry> embed      --input /in/seqs.fasta --output /out
47	                   [--pooling mean|cls|none] [--layers -1] [--batch-size N] [--device cpu|cuda]
48	
49	<entry> likelihood --input /in/seqs.fasta --output /out
50	                   [--batch-size N] [--device cpu|cuda]
51	
52	<entry> score      --input /in/variants.csv --output /out
53	                   [--method masked-marginal|wt-marginal] [--batch-size N] [--device cpu|cuda]
54	
55	<entry> generate   --input /in/prompts.fasta --output /out
56	                   [--num-samples N] [--temperature T] [--top-p P] [--max-length L]
57	                   [--seed S] [--batch-size N] [--device cpu|cuda]
58	```
59	
60	**Flag conventions**
61	
62	- `--input` — a path inside the container, always under the read-only `/in`.
63	- `--output` — a directory inside the container, always the writable `/out`.
64	- `--pooling` — `mean`/`cls` produce one pooled vector per record; `none`
65	  produces per-residue arrays. The manifest declares which modes are supported.
66	- `--layers` — comma-separated transformer layer indices; negative indexing
67	  allowed (`-1` = last). At `0.1` a single index is the supported path.
68	- `--device` — `cuda` is only valid when the container was launched with
69	  `--gpus`. If `cuda` is requested but unavailable, the image emits a
70	  `DeviceUnavailable` error (section 6).
71	
72	The `protlms` client always launches with mounts `-v <host_in>:/in:ro` and
73	`-v <host_out>:/out:rw`, and passes `--gpus all` when a GPU is requested.
74	
75	---
76	
77	## 3. Manifest schema
78	
79	Emitted as JSON on stdout by the `manifest` subcommand. Mirrors
80	`protlms.contract.Manifest`.
81	
82	| Field | Type | Notes |
83	|---|---|---|
84	| `contract_version` | string | `MAJOR.MINOR`. |
85	| `name` | string | Canonical model name (e.g. `esm2_t6_8M`). |
86	| `version` | string | Model/image semantic version. |
87	| `description` | string | Human-readable summary. |
88	| `model_family` | string | e.g. `esm2`. |
89	| `capabilities` | string[] | Subset of `embed`, `likelihood`, `score`, `generate`. |
90	| `embedding_dim` | int | Representation width. |
91	| `max_sequence_length` | int | Longer inputs are truncated (recorded in `warnings`). |
92	| `pooling_modes` | string[] | Subset of `mean`, `cls`, `none`. Empty if the model does not support `embed`. |
93	| `num_layers` | int | Transformer layer count (bounds `--layers`). |
94	| `min_gpu_memory_gb` | float \| null | `null` ⇒ runs comfortably on CPU. |
95	| `default_batch_size` | int | Used when `--batch-size` is omitted. |
96	| `image_digest` | string \| null | Optional. **Resolved by the client at run time** (`docker image inspect`); images need not self-report it. |
97	
98	**Worked example** ([`tests/data/manifest.example.json`](../tests/data/manifest.example.json)):
99	
100	```json
101	{
102	  "contract_version": "0.3",
103	  "name": "esm2_t6_8M",
104	  "version": "1.0.0",
105	  "description": "ESM2 8M-parameter masked protein language model.",
106	  "model_family": "esm2",
107	  "capabilities": ["embed", "likelihood", "score"],
108	  "embedding_dim": 320,
109	  "max_sequence_length": 1024,
110	  "pooling_modes": ["mean", "cls", "none"],
111	  "num_layers": 6,
112	  "min_gpu_memory_gb": null,
113	  "default_batch_size": 8
114	}
115	```
116	
117	---
118	
119	## 4. Input & output conventions
120	
121	### Inputs
122	
123	- `embed` / `likelihood`: a FASTA file at `/in/seqs.fasta`. The client
124	  normalizes headers so each record header is just its id token.
125	- `generate`: a FASTA file at `/in/prompts.fasta` where each record's sequence
126	  is treated as a prefix. An empty sequence triggers unconditional sampling.
127	- `score`: a CSV at `/in/variants.csv` with the schema described below.
128	
129	### Outputs
130	
131	Every successful run writes `/out/result.json` (section 5) plus the artifact
132	files it describes. The client reads `result.json` to discover outputs rather
133	than globbing the directory.
134	
135	| Capability | Files written under `/out` |
136	|---|---|
137	| `embed`, pooled (`mean`/`cls`) | `embeddings.npz` — one `(embedding_dim,)` float32 array per record, keyed by record id. |
138	| `embed`, per-residue (`none`) | `per_residue/<id>.npy` — one `(L, embedding_dim)` float32 array per record. |
139	| `likelihood` | `likelihoods.csv` (schema below). |
140	| `score` | `scores.csv` (schema below). |
141	| `generate` | `generated.fasta` — clean amino-acid sequences (control/special tokens stripped). Headers are `{prompt_id}__sample{k}` for k=0..num_samples-1. |
142	
143	**Record ids** are sanitized for use as filenames / npz keys: characters
144	outside `[A-Za-z0-9._-]` become `_`, and collisions are de-duplicated with a
145	`__N` suffix. With clean ids (the common case) the sanitized id equals the
146	original.
147	
148	**`likelihoods.csv` columns:**
149	
150	| Column | Meaning |
151	|---|---|
152	| `record_id` | The (sanitized) record id. |
153	| `seq_len` | Number of scored residues. |
154	| `log_likelihood` | Σ over positions of log P(true residue). |
155	| `mean_log_likelihood` | `log_likelihood / seq_len` (length-normalized). |
156	| `perplexity` | `exp(-mean_log_likelihood)`. |
157	
158	The scoring method is recorded in `result.json` under `params.likelihood_method`,
159	whose value is `"masked_marginal"` (masked LMs such as ESM2) or `"causal"`
160	(autoregressive LMs such as ProGen2).
161	
162	**`variants.csv` input columns (for `score`):**
163	
164	| Column | Meaning |
165	|---|---|
166	| `variant_id` | A unique identifier for the variant. |
167	| `wt_sequence` | The wild-type reference sequence (full length). |
168	| `mutant` | A mutation descriptor; 1-indexed notation `{WT}{pos}{MUT}` or colon-separated multi-mutants (`A24G:T56S`). A self-substitution (e.g. `A24A`) scores exactly 0. |
169	
170	**`scores.csv` output columns (for `score`):**
171	
172	| Column | Meaning |
173	|---|---|
174	| `variant_id` | From the input; allows cross-referencing. |
175	| `mutant` | The mutation descriptor from the input. |
176	| `n_mutations` | Number of individual mutations in the descriptor (1 for `A24G`, 2 for `A24G:T56S`). |
177	| `score` | The score value; blank if the variant is invalid (WT-residue mismatch, out-of-range position, or malformed descriptor). See `result.warnings`. |
178	
179	---
180	
181	## 5. `result.json` schema
182	
183	The success summary written to `/out/result.json`. Mirrors
184	`protlms.contract.Result` + `OutputArtifact`.
185	
186	| Field | Type | Notes |
187	|---|---|---|
188	| `contract_version` | string | |
189	| `capability` | string | The capability that produced this output. |
190	| `model_name` | string | |
191	| `n_input_records` | int | |
192	| `n_output_records` | int | May differ from input if records were skipped. |
193	| `artifacts` | OutputArtifact[] | See below. |
194	| `warnings` | string[] | e.g. truncation notices. Defaults to `[]`. |
195	| `params` | object(string→string) | Echo of the request params for reproducibility. |
196	
197	**OutputArtifact**
198	
199	| Field | Type | Notes |
200	|---|---|---|
201	| `path` | string | Relative to `/out`. |
202	| `kind` | string | `pooled_embeddings`, `per_residue_embeddings`, `likelihoods_csv`, `variant_scores_csv`, or `generated_fasta` (free string for forward compatibility). |
203	| `record_ids` | string[] \| null | Records contained in this artifact. |
204	| `shape` | int[] \| null | Logical array shape, if applicable. |
205	| `dtype` | string \| null | e.g. `float32`. |
206	
207	**Worked example (embed)** ([`tests/data/result.embed.example.json`](../tests/data/result.embed.example.json)):
208	
209	```json
210	{
211	  "contract_version": "0.3",
212	  "capability": "embed",
213	  "model_name": "esm2_t6_8M",
214	  "n_input_records": 3,
215	  "n_output_records": 3,
216	  "artifacts": [
217	    {
218	      "path": "embeddings.npz",
219	      "kind": "pooled_embeddings",
220	      "record_ids": ["insulin_b", "gb1", "melittin"],
221	      "shape": [3, 320],
222	      "dtype": "float32"
223	    }
224	  ],
225	  "warnings": [],
226	  "params": {"pooling": "mean", "layers": "-1"}
227	}
228	```
229	
230	**Worked example (score)** ([`tests/data/result.score.example.json`](../tests/data/result.score.example.json)):
231	
232	```json
233	{
234	  "contract_version": "0.3",
235	  "capability": "score",
236	  "model_name": "esm2_t6_8M",
237	  "n_input_records": 3,
238	  "n_output_records": 3,
239	  "artifacts": [
240	    {
241	      "path": "scores.csv",
242	      "kind": "variant_scores_csv",
243	      "record_ids": ["self", "single", "double"]
244	    }
245	  ],
246	  "warnings": [],
247	  "params": {"method": "masked-marginal"}
248	}
249	```
250	
251	**Worked example (generate)** ([`tests/data/result.generate.example.json`](../tests/data/result.generate.example.json)):
252	
253	```json
254	{
255	  "contract_version": "0.3",
256	  "capability": "generate",
257	  "model_name": "progen2-small",
258	  "n_input_records": 2,
259	  "n_output_records": 4,
260	  "artifacts": [
261	    {"path": "generated.fasta", "kind": "generated_fasta",
262	     "record_ids": ["prompt1__sample0", "prompt1__sample1", "uncond__sample0", "uncond__sample1"]}
263	  ],
264	  "warnings": [],
265	  "params": {"num_samples": "2", "temperature": "0.8", "top_p": "0.9", "seed": "42"}
266	}
267	```
268	
269	---
270	
271	## 6. Error contract
272	
273	- **Success:** exit code `0` **and** `/out/result.json` present.
274	- **Failure:** non-zero exit code. The image writes a single JSON object as the
275	  last line of **stderr**, matching `protlms.contract.ContainerError`:
276	
277	```json
278	{"contract_version": "0.3", "error_type": "SequenceTooLong",
279	 "message": "sequence 'seq1' exceeds max_sequence_length", "details": {"id": "seq1"}}
280	```
281	
282	| Field | Type | Notes |
283	|---|---|---|
284	| `contract_version` | string \| null | |
285	| `error_type` | string | e.g. `InvalidInput`, `UnsupportedCapability`, `SequenceTooLong`, `DeviceUnavailable`, `InternalError`. |
286	| `message` | string | Human-readable. |
287	| `details` | object(string→string) | Free-form context. Defaults to `{}`. |
288	
289	The client scans stderr from the last line backwards for the first valid error
290	object and raises `ContainerExecutionError` carrying its fields, the exit code,
291	and a tail of stderr. If no structured error is found (e.g. a segfault), it
292	raises with the raw stderr tail so the failure is still legible.
293	
294	---
295	
296	## 7. Adding a model
297	
298	1. Create `containers/<family>/` with a `Dockerfile` and an entrypoint that
299	   implements the subcommands above for the capabilities you support.
300	2. Bake weights into the image at build time (reproducible, offline runtime).
301	3. Emit a manifest whose `contract_version` matches a major version the client
302	   supports.
303	4. Register the image in [`src/protlms/_data/models.yaml`](../src/protlms/_data/models.yaml).
304	
305	The client does not change. See [`containers/esm2/`](../containers/esm2/) for a
306	reference implementation.
307

# The protlms Container Contract

> **Contract version:** `0.3`
>
> This document is the agreement between the `protlms` client and every model
> image. It is the *only* thing the two sides share. Its schemas are mirrored
> exactly by the Pydantic models in [`src/protlms/contract.py`](../src/protlms/contract.py);
> **edit the two together.** The worked examples here are checked in under
> [`tests/data/`](../tests/data/) and validated by `tests/test_contract.py`, so a
> drift between this document and the code fails a test.

A model image is a black box that exposes a small command-line interface. The
client never imports model code, never knows a model's tokenizer, and never
sees its weights. It resolves a model name to an image, runs the image with a
read-only input mount and a writable output mount, and parses the files the
image leaves behind.

---

## 1. Versioning & compatibility

`contract_version` is a `MAJOR.MINOR` string. The client refuses to run against
an image whose **major** differs from its own.

| Image vs. client | Behavior |
|---|---|
| Same major, image minor ≤ client minor | Run normally. |
| Same major, image minor > client minor | Run; the client may warn. Unknown manifest/result fields are ignored. |
| Different major | Refuse with `ContractVersionError`. |

Forward compatibility relies on the client ignoring unknown fields
(`extra="ignore"` on every model). Adding a new capability or field is a
**minor** bump; changing or removing existing semantics is a **major** bump.

---

## 2. Internal CLI

The image's `ENTRYPOINT` is the contract CLI. It MUST expose these subcommands:

```
<entry> manifest
        # Print the manifest JSON (section 3) to stdout and exit 0.
        # Requires no mounts — introspectable with a one-shot `docker run`.

<entry> embed      --input /in/seqs.fasta --output /out
                   [--pooling mean|cls|none] [--layers -1] [--batch-size N] [--device cpu|cuda]

<entry> likelihood --input /in/seqs.fasta --output /out
                   [--batch-size N] [--device cpu|cuda]

<entry> score      --input /in/variants.csv --output /out
                   [--method masked-marginal|wt-marginal] [--batch-size N] [--device cpu|cuda]

<entry> generate   --input /in/prompts.fasta --output /out
                   [--num-samples N] [--temperature T] [--top-p P] [--max-length L]
                   [--seed S] [--batch-size N] [--device cpu|cuda]
```

**Flag conventions**

- `--input` — a path inside the container, always under the read-only `/in`.
- `--output` — a directory inside the container, always the writable `/out`.
- `--pooling` — `mean`/`cls` produce one pooled vector per record; `none`
  produces per-residue arrays. The manifest declares which modes are supported.
- `--layers` — comma-separated transformer layer indices; negative indexing
  allowed (`-1` = last). At `0.1` a single index is the supported path.
- `--device` — `cuda` is only valid when the container was launched with
  `--gpus`. If `cuda` is requested but unavailable, the image emits a
  `DeviceUnavailable` error (section 6).

The `protlms` client always launches with mounts `-v <host_in>:/in:ro` and
`-v <host_out>:/out:rw`, and passes `--gpus all` when a GPU is requested.

---

## 3. Manifest schema

Emitted as JSON on stdout by the `manifest` subcommand. Mirrors
`protlms.contract.Manifest`.

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `contract_version` | string | `MAJOR.MINOR`. |
| `name` | string | Canonical model name (e.g. `esm2_t6_8M`). |
| `version` | string | Model/image semantic version. |
| `description` | string | Human-readable summary. |
| `model_family` | string | e.g. `esm2`. |
| `capabilities` | string[] | Subset of `embed`, `likelihood`, `score`, `generate`. |
| `embedding_dim` | int | Representation width. |
| `max_sequence_length` | int | Longer inputs are truncated (recorded in `warnings`). |
| `pooling_modes` | string[] | Subset of `mean`, `cls`, `none`. Empty if the model does not support `embed`. |
| `num_layers` | int | Transformer layer count (bounds `--layers`). |
| `min_gpu_memory_gb` | float \| null | `null` ⇒ runs comfortably on CPU. |
| `default_batch_size` | int | Used when `--batch-size` is omitted. |
| `image_digest` | string \| null | Optional. **Resolved by the client at run time** (`docker image inspect`); images need not self-report it. |

**Worked example** ([`tests/data/manifest.example.json`](../tests/data/manifest.example.json)):

```json
{
  "contract_version": "0.3",
  "name": "esm2_t6_8M",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "ESM2 8M-parameter masked protein language model.",
  "model_family": "esm2",
  "capabilities": ["embed", "likelihood", "score"],
  "embedding_dim": 320,
  "max_sequence_length": 1024,
  "pooling_modes": ["mean", "cls", "none"],
  "num_layers": 6,
  "min_gpu_memory_gb": null,
  "default_batch_size": 8
}
```

---

## 4. Input & output conventions

### Inputs

- `embed` / `likelihood`: a FASTA file at `/in/seqs.fasta`. The client
  normalizes headers so each record header is just its id token.
- `generate`: a FASTA file at `/in/prompts.fasta` where each record's sequence
  is treated as a prefix. An empty sequence triggers unconditional sampling.
- `score`: a CSV at `/in/variants.csv` with the schema described below.

### Outputs

Every successful run writes `/out/result.json` (section 5) plus the artifact
files it describes. The client reads `result.json` to discover outputs rather
than globbing the directory.

| Capability | Files written under `/out` |
|---|---|
| `embed`, pooled (`mean`/`cls`) | `embeddings.npz` — one `(embedding_dim,)` float32 array per record, keyed by record id. |
| `embed`, per-residue (`none`) | `per_residue/<id>.npy` — one `(L, embedding_dim)` float32 array per record. |
| `likelihood` | `likelihoods.csv` (schema below). |
| `score` | `scores.csv` (schema below). |
| `generate` | `generated.fasta` — clean amino-acid sequences (control/special tokens stripped). Headers are `{prompt_id}__sample{k}` for k=0..num_samples-1. |

**Record ids** are sanitized for use as filenames / npz keys: characters
outside `[A-Za-z0-9._-]` become `_`, and collisions are de-duplicated with a
`__N` suffix. With clean ids (the common case) the sanitized id equals the
original.

**`likelihoods.csv` columns:**

| Column | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `record_id` | The (sanitized) record id. |
| `seq_len` | Number of scored residues. |
| `log_likelihood` | Σ over positions of log P(true residue). |
| `mean_log_likelihood` | `log_likelihood / seq_len` (length-normalized). |
| `perplexity` | `exp(-mean_log_likelihood)`. |

The scoring method is recorded in `result.json` under `params.likelihood_method`,
whose value is `"masked_marginal"` (masked LMs such as ESM2) or `"causal"`
(autoregressive LMs such as ProGen2).

**`variants.csv` input columns (for `score`):**

| Column | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `variant_id` | A unique identifier for the variant. |
| `wt_sequence` | The wild-type reference sequence (full length). |
| `mutant` | A mutation descriptor; 1-indexed notation `{WT}{pos}{MUT}` or colon-separated multi-mutants (`A24G:T56S`). A self-substitution (e.g. `A24A`) scores exactly 0. |

**`scores.csv` output columns (for `score`):**

| Column | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `variant_id` | From the input; allows cross-referencing. |
| `mutant` | The mutation descriptor from the input. |
| `n_mutations` | Number of individual mutations in the descriptor (1 for `A24G`, 2 for `A24G:T56S`). |
| `score` | The score value; blank if the variant is invalid (WT-residue mismatch, out-of-range position, or malformed descriptor). See `result.warnings`. |

---

## 5. `result.json` schema

The success summary written to `/out/result.json`. Mirrors
`protlms.contract.Result` + `OutputArtifact`.

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `contract_version` | string | |
| `capability` | string | The capability that produced this output. |
| `model_name` | string | |
| `n_input_records` | int | |
| `n_output_records` | int | May differ from input if records were skipped. |
| `artifacts` | OutputArtifact[] | See below. |
| `warnings` | string[] | e.g. truncation notices. Defaults to `[]`. |
| `params` | object(string→string) | Echo of the request params for reproducibility. |

**OutputArtifact**

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `path` | string | Relative to `/out`. |
| `kind` | string | `pooled_embeddings`, `per_residue_embeddings`, `likelihoods_csv`, `variant_scores_csv`, or `generated_fasta` (free string for forward compatibility). |
| `record_ids` | string[] \| null | Records contained in this artifact. |
| `shape` | int[] \| null | Logical array shape, if applicable. |
| `dtype` | string \| null | e.g. `float32`. |

**Worked example (embed)** ([`tests/data/result.embed.example.json`](../tests/data/result.embed.example.json)):

```json
{
  "contract_version": "0.3",
  "capability": "embed",
  "model_name": "esm2_t6_8M",
  "n_input_records": 3,
  "n_output_records": 3,
  "artifacts": [
    {
      "path": "embeddings.npz",
      "kind": "pooled_embeddings",
      "record_ids": ["insulin_b", "gb1", "melittin"],
      "shape": [3, 320],
      "dtype": "float32"
    }
  ],
  "warnings": [],
  "params": {"pooling": "mean", "layers": "-1"}
}
```

**Worked example (score)** ([`tests/data/result.score.example.json`](../tests/data/result.score.example.json)):

```json
{
  "contract_version": "0.3",
  "capability": "score",
  "model_name": "esm2_t6_8M",
  "n_input_records": 3,
  "n_output_records": 3,
  "artifacts": [
    {
      "path": "scores.csv",
      "kind": "variant_scores_csv",
      "record_ids": ["self", "single", "double"]
    }
  ],
  "warnings": [],
  "params": {"method": "masked-marginal"}
}
```

**Worked example (generate)** ([`tests/data/result.generate.example.json`](../tests/data/result.generate.example.json)):

```json
{
  "contract_version": "0.3",
  "capability": "generate",
  "model_name": "progen2-small",
  "n_input_records": 2,
  "n_output_records": 4,
  "artifacts": [
    {"path": "generated.fasta", "kind": "generated_fasta",
     "record_ids": ["prompt1__sample0", "prompt1__sample1", "uncond__sample0", "uncond__sample1"]}
  ],
  "warnings": [],
  "params": {"num_samples": "2", "temperature": "0.8", "top_p": "0.9", "seed": "42"}
}
```

---

## 6. Error contract

- **Success:** exit code `0` **and** `/out/result.json` present.
- **Failure:** non-zero exit code. The image writes a single JSON object as the
  last line of **stderr**, matching `protlms.contract.ContainerError`:

```json
{"contract_version": "0.3", "error_type": "SequenceTooLong",
 "message": "sequence 'seq1' exceeds max_sequence_length", "details": {"id": "seq1"}}
```

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `contract_version` | string \| null | |
| `error_type` | string | e.g. `InvalidInput`, `UnsupportedCapability`, `SequenceTooLong`, `DeviceUnavailable`, `InternalError`. |
| `message` | string | Human-readable. |
| `details` | object(string→string) | Free-form context. Defaults to `{}`. |

The client scans stderr from the last line backwards for the first valid error
object and raises `ContainerExecutionError` carrying its fields, the exit code,
and a tail of stderr. If no structured error is found (e.g. a segfault), it
raises with the raw stderr tail so the failure is still legible.

---

## 7. Adding a model

1. Create `containers/<family>/` with a `Dockerfile` and an entrypoint that
   implements the subcommands above for the capabilities you support.
2. Bake weights into the image at build time (reproducible, offline runtime).
3. Emit a manifest whose `contract_version` matches a major version the client
   supports.
4. Register the image in [`src/protlms/_data/models.yaml`](../src/protlms/_data/models.yaml).

The client does not change. See [`containers/esm2/`](../containers/esm2/) for a
reference implementation.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python
2	"""Contract-compliant entrypoint for the ESM-C model image.
3	
4	Implements the protlms container contract (see docs/CONTRACT.md) for the ESM-C
5	masked protein language model via EvolutionaryScale's native ``esm`` SDK. Exposes
6	the ``manifest``, ``embed``, ``likelihood``, and ``score`` subcommands plus a
7	hidden ``_prefetch`` used at build time to bake weights into the image.
8	
9	Heavy imports (``torch``, ``esm``) happen inside the functions that need them, so
10	the pure helpers can be unit-tested without the ML stack installed.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import argparse
16	import json
17	import os
18	import re
19	import sys
20	from pathlib import Path
21	
22	CONTRACT_VERSION = "0.3"
23	MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH = 2048
24	DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE = 8
25	DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT = os.environ.get("ESMC_CHECKPOINT", "esmc_300m")
26	
27	# Architecture facts keyed by checkpoint name. Keeping these here lets `manifest`
28	# stay model-load-free (the checkpoint name pins the architecture, so there is no
29	# drift risk): embedding_dim is the model width, num_layers the transformer depth.
30	_MODEL_INFO: dict[str, dict[str, object]] = {
31	    "esmc_300m": {"embedding_dim": 960, "num_layers": 30, "min_gpu_memory_gb": None},
32	    "esmc_600m": {"embedding_dim": 1152, "num_layers": 36, "min_gpu_memory_gb": 4.0},
33	}
34	
35	_ID_SAFE = re.compile(r"[^A-Za-z0-9._-]")
36	_MUTANT_RE = re.compile(r"^([A-Za-z])(\d+)([A-Za-z])$")
37	
38	
39	# --- pure helpers (unit-testable without torch/esm) ------------------------
40	
41	
42	def sanitize_ids(ids: list[str]) -> list[str]:
43	    """Sanitize record ids for filenames/keys, de-duplicating collisions."""
44	    seen: dict[str, int] = {}
45	    out: list[str] = []
46	    for raw in ids:
47	        clean = _ID_SAFE.sub("_", raw) or "seq"
48	        if clean in seen:
49	            seen[clean] += 1
50	            clean = f"{clean}__{seen[clean]}"
51	        else:
52	            seen[clean] = 0
53	        out.append(clean)
54	    return out
55	
56	
57	def read_fasta(path: Path) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
58	    """Parse a FASTA file into ``(id, sequence)`` tuples."""
59	    records: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
60	    header: str | None = None
61	    chunks: list[str] = []
62	    for raw in Path(path).read_text().splitlines():
63	        line = raw.strip()
64	        if not line:
65	            continue
66	        if line.startswith(">"):
67	            if header is not None:
68	                records.append((header, "".join(chunks).upper()))
69	            header = line[1:].split(maxsplit=1)[0] if line[1:].split() else line[1:]
70	            chunks = []
71	        else:
72	            chunks.append(line)
73	    if header is not None:
74	        records.append((header, "".join(chunks).upper()))
75	    return records
76	
77	
78	def perplexity_from_mean(mean_log_likelihood: float) -> float:
79	    """Pseudo-perplexity from a mean pseudo-log-likelihood."""
80	    import math
81	
82	    return math.exp(-mean_log_likelihood)
83	
84	
85	def parse_mutant(mutant: str) -> list[tuple[str, int, str]]:
86	    """Parse a mutation string like ``A24G`` or ``A24G:T56S`` (1-indexed)."""
87	    subs: list[tuple[str, int, str]] = []
88	    for token in mutant.split(":"):
89	        match = _MUTANT_RE.match(token.strip())
90	        if not match:
91	            raise ValueError(f"invalid mutation token {token!r}")
92	        subs.append((match.group(1).upper(), int(match.group(2)), match.group(3).upper()))
93	    return subs
94	
95	
96	def _valid_positions(group: list[dict], wt_seq: str) -> set[int]:
97	    """Collect in-range mutated positions across a WT group (for logit computation)."""
98	    positions: set[int] = set()
99	    for row in group:
100	        try:
101	            for _wt, pos, _mut in parse_mutant(row["mutant"]):
102	                if 1 <= pos <= len(wt_seq):
103	                    positions.add(pos)
104	        except ValueError:
105	            continue
106	    return positions
107	
108	
109	def _truncate(seq: str, warnings: list[str], record_id: str) -> str:
110	    if len(seq) > MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH:
111	        warnings.append(f"sequence {record_id!r} truncated to {MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH} residues")
112	        return seq[:MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH]
113	    return seq
114	
115	
116	# --- contract I/O ----------------------------------------------------------
117	
118	
119	def write_result(output_dir: Path, payload: dict) -> None:
120	    """Write the ``result.json`` success summary."""
121	    (output_dir / "result.json").write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2))
122	
123	
124	def emit_error_and_exit(error_type: str, message: str, **details: str) -> None:
125	    """Write a structured ContainerError to stderr and exit non-zero."""
126	    error = {
127	        "contract_version": CONTRACT_VERSION,
128	        "error_type": error_type,
129	        "message": message,
130	        "details": {k: str(v) for k, v in details.items()},
131	    }
132	    print(json.dumps(error), file=sys.stderr)
133	    raise SystemExit(1)
134	
135	
136	def build_manifest() -> dict:
137	    """Build the manifest dict from the checkpoint-keyed architecture table."""
138	    info = _MODEL_INFO[DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT]
139	    return {
140	        "contract_version": CONTRACT_VERSION,
141	        "name": DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT,
142	        "version": "1.0.0",
143	        "description": f"ESM-C masked protein language model ({DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT}).",
144	        "model_family": "esm-c",
145	        "capabilities": ["embed", "likelihood", "score"],
146	        "embedding_dim": info["embedding_dim"],
147	        "max_sequence_length": MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH,
148	        "pooling_modes": ["mean", "cls", "none"],
149	        "num_layers": info["num_layers"],
150	        "min_gpu_memory_gb": info["min_gpu_memory_gb"],
151	        "default_batch_size": DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE,
152	    }
153	
154	
155	# --- model helpers ---------------------------------------------------------
156	
157	
158	def pick_device(requested: str | None) -> str:
159	    """Choose the torch device, validating an explicit ``cuda`` request."""
160	    import torch
161	
162	    available = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"
163	    if requested in (None, "auto"):
164	        return available
165	    if requested == "cuda" and available != "cuda":
166	        emit_error_and_exit("DeviceUnavailable", "cuda requested but no GPU is available")
167	    return requested
168	
169	
170	def load_model(device: str):  # noqa: ANN201 - returns an ESMC module
171	    """Load the ESM-C model for the configured checkpoint (flash-attn not installed)."""
172	    import torch
173	    from esm.models.esmc import ESMC
174	
175	    model = ESMC.from_pretrained(
176	        DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT,
177	        device=torch.device(device),
178	    )
179	    model.eval()
180	    return model
181	
182	
183	def _encode_ids(model, seq: str):  # noqa: ANN001, ANN202
184	    """Tokenize a sequence to a 1-D token-id tensor (incl BOS/EOS) on CPU."""
185	    from esm.sdk.api import ESMProtein
186	
187	    tensor = model.encode(ESMProtein(sequence=seq))
188	    return tensor.sequence.cpu()  # (T,) long
189	
190	
191	def _embed_one(model, seq: str, device: str):  # noqa: ANN001, ANN202
192	    """Return (per-residue (L, D), cls (D,)) float32 arrays for one sequence."""
193	    import numpy as np
194	    import torch
195	
196	    ids = _encode_ids(model, seq).unsqueeze(0).to(device)  # (1, T)
197	    with torch.no_grad():
198	        out = model(sequence_tokens=ids)
199	    emb = out.embeddings[0].float().cpu().numpy()  # (T, D)
200	    residue = emb[1 : 1 + len(seq)].astype(np.float32)  # strip BOS/EOS
201	    cls_vec = emb[0].astype(np.float32)
202	    return residue, cls_vec
203	
204	
205	def _pseudo_log_likelihood(model, seq: str, batch_size: int, device: str) -> float:  # noqa: ANN001
206	    """Masked-marginal pseudo-log-likelihood: O(L) masked forward passes."""
207	    import torch
208	
209	    ids = _encode_ids(model, seq)  # (T,)
210	    mask_id = model.tokenizer.mask_token_id
211	    positions = list(range(1, len(ids) - 1))  # residues only
212	    total = 0.0
213	    for start in range(0, len(positions), batch_size):
214	        chunk = positions[start : start + batch_size]
215	        batch = ids.repeat(len(chunk), 1)  # (b, T)
216	        for row, pos in enumerate(chunk):
217	            batch[row, pos] = mask_id
218	        with torch.no_grad():
219	            out = model(sequence_tokens=batch.to(device))
220	        log_probs = torch.log_softmax(out.sequence_logits.float(), dim=-1)
221	        for row, pos in enumerate(chunk):
222	            total += float(log_probs[row, pos, ids[pos]])
223	    return total
224	
225	
226	def _masked_position_logprobs(model, seq, positions, batch_size, device):  # noqa: ANN001
227	    """Map each 1-indexed position to its masked log-softmax vector over the vocab."""
228	    import torch
229	
230	    ids = _encode_ids(model, seq)
231	    mask_id = model.tokenizer.mask_token_id
232	    ordered = sorted(positions)
233	    out: dict[int, object] = {}
234	    for start in range(0, len(ordered), batch_size):
235	        chunk = ordered[start : start + batch_size]
236	        batch = ids.repeat(len(chunk), 1)
237	        for row, pos in enumerate(chunk):
238	            batch[row, pos] = mask_id
239	        with torch.no_grad():
240	            res = model(sequence_tokens=batch.to(device))
241	        log_probs = torch.log_softmax(res.sequence_logits.float(), dim=-1)
242	        for row, pos in enumerate(chunk):
243	            out[pos] = log_probs[row, pos].cpu().numpy()
244	    return out
245	
246	
247	def _wt_position_logprobs(model, seq, positions, device):  # noqa: ANN001
248	    """Map each 1-indexed position to its unmasked (WT-context) log-softmax vector."""
249	    import torch
250	
251	    ids = _encode_ids(model, seq).unsqueeze(0).to(device)
252	    with torch.no_grad():
253	        out = model(sequence_tokens=ids)
254	    log_probs = torch.log_softmax(out.sequence_logits.float(), dim=-1)[0].cpu().numpy()
255	    return {pos: log_probs[pos] for pos in positions}
256	
257	
258	def _score_variant(mutant, wt_seq, logp_by_pos, tokenizer):  # noqa: ANN001
259	    """Return (score, n_mutations, error_message_or_None) for one variant."""
260	    try:
261	        subs = parse_mutant(mutant)
262	    except ValueError as exc:
263	        return None, 0, str(exc)
264	    total = 0.0
265	    for wt_aa, pos, mut_aa in subs:
266	        if not 1 <= pos <= len(wt_seq):
267	            return None, len(subs), f"position {pos} out of range for {mutant}"
268	        if wt_seq[pos - 1] != wt_aa:
269	            return None, len(subs), f"WT residue mismatch at {pos} in {mutant}"
270	        vec = logp_by_pos[pos]
271	        wt_id = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(wt_aa)
272	        mut_id = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(mut_aa)
273	        total += float(vec[mut_id] - vec[wt_id])
274	    return total, len(subs), None
275	
276	
277	# --- subcommands -----------------------------------------------------------
278	
279	
280	def cmd_manifest(_args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:
281	    print(json.dumps(build_manifest()))
282	
283	
284	def cmd_embed(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:
285	    import numpy as np
286	
287	    layer = int(args.layers.split(",")[0])
288	    if layer != -1:
289	        emit_error_and_exit(
290	            "InvalidInput",
291	            "esm-c image supports only the final layer (--layers -1)",
292	            layers=args.layers,
293	        )
294	    device = pick_device(args.device)
295	    model = load_model(device)
296	    records = read_fasta(Path(args.input))
297	    ids = sanitize_ids([rid for rid, _ in records])
298	    output_dir = Path(args.output)
299	    warnings: list[str] = []
300	
301	    pooled: dict[str, object] = {}
302	    artifacts: list[dict] = []
303	    per_residue_dir = output_dir / "per_residue"
304	    if args.pooling == "none":
305	        per_residue_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
306	
307	    dim = _MODEL_INFO[DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT]["embedding_dim"]
308	    for clean_id, (rid, seq) in zip(ids, records, strict=True):
309	        seq = _truncate(seq, warnings, rid)
310	        residue, cls_vec = _embed_one(model, seq, device)
311	        if args.pooling == "mean":
312	            pooled[clean_id] = residue.mean(axis=0)
313	        elif args.pooling == "cls":
314	            pooled[clean_id] = cls_vec
315	        else:  # none
316	            _save_npy(per_residue_dir / f"{clean_id}.npy", residue)
317	            artifacts.append(
318	                {
319	                    "path": f"per_residue/{clean_id}.npy",
320	                    "kind": "per_residue_embeddings",
321	                    "record_ids": [clean_id],
322	                    "shape": list(residue.shape),
323	                    "dtype": "float32",
324	                }
325	            )
326	
327	    if args.pooling in ("mean", "cls"):
328	        np.savez(output_dir / "embeddings.npz", **pooled)
329	        artifacts.append(
330	            {
331	                "path": "embeddings.npz",
332	                "kind": "pooled_embeddings",
333	                "record_ids": ids,
334	                "shape": [len(ids), dim],
335	                "dtype": "float32",
336	            }
337	        )
338	    _write_capability_result(output_dir, "embed", records, artifacts, warnings, args)
339	
340	
341	def cmd_likelihood(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:
342	    device = pick_device(args.device)
343	    model = load_model(device)
344	    batch_size = args.batch_size or DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE
345	    records = read_fasta(Path(args.input))
346	    ids = sanitize_ids([rid for rid, _ in records])
347	    output_dir = Path(args.output)
348	    warnings: list[str] = []
349	
350	    rows = ["record_id,seq_len,log_likelihood,mean_log_likelihood,perplexity"]
351	    for clean_id, (rid, seq) in zip(ids, records, strict=True):
352	        seq = _truncate(seq, warnings, rid)
353	        pll = _pseudo_log_likelihood(model, seq, batch_size, device)
354	        mean = pll / max(len(seq), 1)
355	        rows.append(f"{clean_id},{len(seq)},{pll:.6f},{mean:.6f},{perplexity_from_mean(mean):.6f}")
356	    (output_dir / "likelihoods.csv").write_text("\n".join(rows) + "\n")
357	
358	    artifacts = [{"path": "likelihoods.csv", "kind": "likelihoods_csv", "record_ids": ids}]
359	    _write_capability_result(output_dir, "likelihood", records, artifacts, warnings, args)
360	
361	
362	def cmd_score(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:
363	    """Score variants (masked-marginal or wt-marginal) from a CSV input."""
364	    import csv as csv_module
365	
366	    device = pick_device(args.device)
367	    model = load_model(device)
368	    batch_size = args.batch_size or DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE
369	    with Path(args.input).open(newline="") as handle:
370	        rows = list(csv_module.DictReader(handle))
371	    warnings: list[str] = []
372	    groups: dict[str, list[dict]] = {}
373	    for row in rows:
374	        groups.setdefault(row["wt_sequence"], []).append(row)
375	
376	    out_rows: list[list[object]] = []
377	    for wt_seq, group in groups.items():
378	        seq = _truncate(wt_seq, warnings, "<wt>")
379	        positions = _valid_positions(group, seq)
380	        if args.method == "wt-marginal":
381	            logp = _wt_position_logprobs(model, seq, positions, device)
382	        else:
383	            logp = _masked_position_logprobs(model, seq, positions, batch_size, device)
384	        for row in group:
385	            score, n_mut, err = _score_variant(row["mutant"], seq, logp, model.tokenizer)
386	            if err is not None:
387	                warnings.append(f"{row['variant_id']}: {err}")
388	            score_str = "" if score is None else f"{score:.6f}"
389	            out_rows.append([row["variant_id"], row["mutant"], n_mut, score_str])
390	
391	    output_dir = Path(args.output)
392	    with (output_dir / "scores.csv").open("w", newline="") as handle:
393	        writer = csv_module.writer(handle)
394	        writer.writerow(["variant_id", "mutant", "n_mutations", "score"])
395	        writer.writerows(out_rows)
396	    artifacts = [
397	        {
398	            "path": "scores.csv",
399	            "kind": "variant_scores_csv",
400	            "record_ids": [r["variant_id"] for r in rows],
401	        }
402	    ]
403	    write_result(
404	        output_dir,
405	        {
406	            "contract_version": CONTRACT_VERSION,
407	            "capability": "score",
408	            "model_name": DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT,
409	            "n_input_records": len(rows),
410	            "n_output_records": len(rows),
411	            "artifacts": artifacts,
412	            "warnings": warnings,
413	            "params": {"method": args.method, "device": args.device or "auto"},
414	        },
415	    )
416	
417	
418	def cmd_prefetch(_args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:
419	    """Bake weights into the image at build time (populate the HF cache)."""
420	    import torch
421	    from esm.models.esmc import ESMC
422	
423	    ESMC.from_pretrained(
424	        DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT,
425	        device=torch.device("cpu"),
426	    )
427	    print(f"prefetched {DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT}")
428	
429	
430	# --- shared result writer + arg parsing ------------------------------------
431	
432	
433	def _save_npy(path: Path, array) -> None:  # noqa: ANN001
434	    import numpy as np
435	
436	    np.save(path, array.astype(np.float32))
437	
438	
439	def _write_capability_result(
440	    output_dir: Path,
441	    capability: str,
442	    records: list,
443	    artifacts: list[dict],
444	    warnings: list[str],
445	    args: argparse.Namespace,
446	) -> None:
447	    params = {"device": args.device or "auto"}
448	    if capability == "embed":
449	        params |= {"pooling": args.pooling, "layers": args.layers}
450	    elif capability == "likelihood":
451	        params |= {"likelihood_method": "masked_marginal"}
452	    write_result(
453	        output_dir,
454	        {
455	            "contract_version": CONTRACT_VERSION,
456	            "capability": capability,
457	            "model_name": DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT,
458	            "n_input_records": len(records),
459	            "n_output_records": len(records),
460	            "artifacts": artifacts,
461	            "warnings": warnings,
462	            "params": params,
463	        },
464	    )
465	
466	
467	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
468	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="esm-c", description="ESM-C protlms contract entrypoint.")
469	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)
470	
471	    sub.add_parser("manifest").set_defaults(func=cmd_manifest)
472	
473	    embed = sub.add_parser("embed")
474	    embed.add_argument("--input", required=True)
475	    embed.add_argument("--output", required=True)
476	    embed.add_argument("--pooling", default="mean", choices=["mean", "cls", "none"])
477	    embed.add_argument("--layers", default="-1")
478	    embed.add_argument("--batch-size", type=int, default=None, dest="batch_size")
479	    embed.add_argument("--device", default=None, choices=["cpu", "cuda", "auto"])
480	    embed.set_defaults(func=cmd_embed)
481	
482	    likelihood = sub.add_parser("likelihood")
483	    likelihood.add_argument("--input", required=True)
484	    likelihood.add_argument("--output", required=True)
485	    likelihood.add_argument("--batch-size", type=int, default=None, dest="batch_size")
486	    likelihood.add_argument("--device", default=None, choices=["cpu", "cuda", "auto"])
487	    likelihood.set_defaults(func=cmd_likelihood)
488	
489	    score = sub.add_parser("score")
490	    score.add_argument("--input", required=True)
491	    score.add_argument("--output", required=True)
492	    score.add_argument(
493	        "--method", default="masked-marginal", choices=["masked-marginal", "wt-marginal"]
494	    )
495	    score.add_argument("--batch-size", type=int, default=None, dest="batch_size")
496	    score.add_argument("--device", default=None, choices=["cpu", "cuda", "auto"])
497	    score.set_defaults(func=cmd_score)
498	
499	    sub.add_parser("_prefetch").set_defaults(func=cmd_prefetch)
500	    return parser
501	
502	
503	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> None:
504	    args = build_parser().parse_args(argv)
505	    try:
506	        args.func(args)
507	    except SystemExit:
508	        raise
509	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - top-level: report as a structured error
510	        emit_error_and_exit("InternalError", str(exc), exception=type(exc).__name__)
511	
512	
513	if __name__ == "__main__":

#!/usr/bin/env python
"""Contract-compliant entrypoint for the ESM-C model image.

Implements the protlms container contract (see docs/CONTRACT.md) for the ESM-C
masked protein language model via EvolutionaryScale's native ``esm`` SDK. Exposes
the ``manifest``, ``embed``, ``likelihood``, and ``score`` subcommands plus a
hidden ``_prefetch`` used at build time to bake weights into the image.

Heavy imports (``torch``, ``esm``) happen inside the functions that need them, so
the pure helpers can be unit-tested without the ML stack installed.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import os
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path

CONTRACT_VERSION = "0.3"
MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH = 2048
DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE = 8
DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT = os.environ.get("ESMC_CHECKPOINT", "esmc_300m")

# Architecture facts keyed by checkpoint name. Keeping these here lets `manifest`
# stay model-load-free (the checkpoint name pins the architecture, so there is no
# drift risk): embedding_dim is the model width, num_layers the transformer depth.
_MODEL_INFO: dict[str, dict[str, object]] = {
    "esmc_300m": {"embedding_dim": 960, "num_layers": 30, "min_gpu_memory_gb": None},
    "esmc_600m": {"embedding_dim": 1152, "num_layers": 36, "min_gpu_memory_gb": 4.0},
}

_ID_SAFE = re.compile(r"[^A-Za-z0-9._-]")
_MUTANT_RE = re.compile(r"^([A-Za-z])(\d+)([A-Za-z])$")


# --- pure helpers (unit-testable without torch/esm) ------------------------


def sanitize_ids(ids: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    """Sanitize record ids for filenames/keys, de-duplicating collisions."""
    seen: dict[str, int] = {}
    out: list[str] = []
    for raw in ids:
        clean = _ID_SAFE.sub("_", raw) or "seq"
        if clean in seen:
            seen[clean] += 1
            clean = f"{clean}__{seen[clean]}"
        else:
            seen[clean] = 0
        out.append(clean)
    return out


def read_fasta(path: Path) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
    """Parse a FASTA file into ``(id, sequence)`` tuples."""
    records: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
    header: str | None = None
    chunks: list[str] = []
    for raw in Path(path).read_text().splitlines():
        line = raw.strip()
        if not line:
            continue
        if line.startswith(">"):
            if header is not None:
                records.append((header, "".join(chunks).upper()))
            header = line[1:].split(maxsplit=1)[0] if line[1:].split() else line[1:]
            chunks = []
        else:
            chunks.append(line)
    if header is not None:
        records.append((header, "".join(chunks).upper()))
    return records


def perplexity_from_mean(mean_log_likelihood: float) -> float:
    """Pseudo-perplexity from a mean pseudo-log-likelihood."""
    import math

    return math.exp(-mean_log_likelihood)


def parse_mutant(mutant: str) -> list[tuple[str, int, str]]:
    """Parse a mutation string like ``A24G`` or ``A24G:T56S`` (1-indexed)."""
    subs: list[tuple[str, int, str]] = []
    for token in mutant.split(":"):
        match = _MUTANT_RE.match(token.strip())
        if not match:
            raise ValueError(f"invalid mutation token {token!r}")
        subs.append((match.group(1).upper(), int(match.group(2)), match.group(3).upper()))
    return subs


def _valid_positions(group: list[dict], wt_seq: str) -> set[int]:
    """Collect in-range mutated positions across a WT group (for logit computation)."""
    positions: set[int] = set()
    for row in group:
        try:
            for _wt, pos, _mut in parse_mutant(row["mutant"]):
                if 1 <= pos <= len(wt_seq):
                    positions.add(pos)
        except ValueError:
            continue
    return positions


def _truncate(seq: str, warnings: list[str], record_id: str) -> str:
    if len(seq) > MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH:
        warnings.append(f"sequence {record_id!r} truncated to {MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH} residues")
        return seq[:MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH]
    return seq


# --- contract I/O ----------------------------------------------------------


def write_result(output_dir: Path, payload: dict) -> None:
    """Write the ``result.json`` success summary."""
    (output_dir / "result.json").write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2))


def emit_error_and_exit(error_type: str, message: str, **details: str) -> None:
    """Write a structured ContainerError to stderr and exit non-zero."""
    error = {
        "contract_version": CONTRACT_VERSION,
        "error_type": error_type,
        "message": message,
        "details": {k: str(v) for k, v in details.items()},
    }
    print(json.dumps(error), file=sys.stderr)
    raise SystemExit(1)


def build_manifest() -> dict:
    """Build the manifest dict from the checkpoint-keyed architecture table."""
    info = _MODEL_INFO[DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT]
    return {
        "contract_version": CONTRACT_VERSION,
        "name": DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT,
        "version": "1.0.0",
        "description": f"ESM-C masked protein language model ({DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT}).",
        "model_family": "esm-c",
        "capabilities": ["embed", "likelihood", "score"],
        "embedding_dim": info["embedding_dim"],
        "max_sequence_length": MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH,
        "pooling_modes": ["mean", "cls", "none"],
        "num_layers": info["num_layers"],
        "min_gpu_memory_gb": info["min_gpu_memory_gb"],
        "default_batch_size": DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE,
    }


# --- model helpers ---------------------------------------------------------


def pick_device(requested: str | None) -> str:
    """Choose the torch device, validating an explicit ``cuda`` request."""
    import torch

    available = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"
    if requested in (None, "auto"):
        return available
    if requested == "cuda" and available != "cuda":
        emit_error_and_exit("DeviceUnavailable", "cuda requested but no GPU is available")
    return requested


def load_model(device: str):  # noqa: ANN201 - returns an ESMC module
    """Load the ESM-C model for the configured checkpoint (flash-attn not installed)."""
    import torch
    from esm.models.esmc import ESMC

    model = ESMC.from_pretrained(
        DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT,
        device=torch.device(device),
    )
    model.eval()
    return model


def _encode_ids(model, seq: str):  # noqa: ANN001, ANN202
    """Tokenize a sequence to a 1-D token-id tensor (incl BOS/EOS) on CPU."""
    from esm.sdk.api import ESMProtein

    tensor = model.encode(ESMProtein(sequence=seq))
    return tensor.sequence.cpu()  # (T,) long


def _embed_one(model, seq: str, device: str):  # noqa: ANN001, ANN202
    """Return (per-residue (L, D), cls (D,)) float32 arrays for one sequence."""
    import numpy as np
    import torch

    ids = _encode_ids(model, seq).unsqueeze(0).to(device)  # (1, T)
    with torch.no_grad():
        out = model(sequence_tokens=ids)
    emb = out.embeddings[0].float().cpu().numpy()  # (T, D)
    residue = emb[1 : 1 + len(seq)].astype(np.float32)  # strip BOS/EOS
    cls_vec = emb[0].astype(np.float32)
    return residue, cls_vec


def _pseudo_log_likelihood(model, seq: str, batch_size: int, device: str) -> float:  # noqa: ANN001
    """Masked-marginal pseudo-log-likelihood: O(L) masked forward passes."""
    import torch

    ids = _encode_ids(model, seq)  # (T,)
    mask_id = model.tokenizer.mask_token_id
    positions = list(range(1, len(ids) - 1))  # residues only
    total = 0.0
    for start in range(0, len(positions), batch_size):
        chunk = positions[start : start + batch_size]
        batch = ids.repeat(len(chunk), 1)  # (b, T)
        for row, pos in enumerate(chunk):
            batch[row, pos] = mask_id
        with torch.no_grad():
            out = model(sequence_tokens=batch.to(device))
        log_probs = torch.log_softmax(out.sequence_logits.float(), dim=-1)
        for row, pos in enumerate(chunk):
            total += float(log_probs[row, pos, ids[pos]])
    return total


def _masked_position_logprobs(model, seq, positions, batch_size, device):  # noqa: ANN001
    """Map each 1-indexed position to its masked log-softmax vector over the vocab."""
    import torch

    ids = _encode_ids(model, seq)
    mask_id = model.tokenizer.mask_token_id
    ordered = sorted(positions)
    out: dict[int, object] = {}
    for start in range(0, len(ordered), batch_size):
        chunk = ordered[start : start + batch_size]
        batch = ids.repeat(len(chunk), 1)
        for row, pos in enumerate(chunk):
            batch[row, pos] = mask_id
        with torch.no_grad():
            res = model(sequence_tokens=batch.to(device))
        log_probs = torch.log_softmax(res.sequence_logits.float(), dim=-1)
        for row, pos in enumerate(chunk):
            out[pos] = log_probs[row, pos].cpu().numpy()
    return out


def _wt_position_logprobs(model, seq, positions, device):  # noqa: ANN001
    """Map each 1-indexed position to its unmasked (WT-context) log-softmax vector."""
    import torch

    ids = _encode_ids(model, seq).unsqueeze(0).to(device)
    with torch.no_grad():
        out = model(sequence_tokens=ids)
    log_probs = torch.log_softmax(out.sequence_logits.float(), dim=-1)[0].cpu().numpy()
    return {pos: log_probs[pos] for pos in positions}


def _score_variant(mutant, wt_seq, logp_by_pos, tokenizer):  # noqa: ANN001
    """Return (score, n_mutations, error_message_or_None) for one variant."""
    try:
        subs = parse_mutant(mutant)
    except ValueError as exc:
        return None, 0, str(exc)
    total = 0.0
    for wt_aa, pos, mut_aa in subs:
        if not 1 <= pos <= len(wt_seq):
            return None, len(subs), f"position {pos} out of range for {mutant}"
        if wt_seq[pos - 1] != wt_aa:
            return None, len(subs), f"WT residue mismatch at {pos} in {mutant}"
        vec = logp_by_pos[pos]
        wt_id = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(wt_aa)
        mut_id = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(mut_aa)
        total += float(vec[mut_id] - vec[wt_id])
    return total, len(subs), None


# --- subcommands -----------------------------------------------------------


def cmd_manifest(_args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:
    print(json.dumps(build_manifest()))


def cmd_embed(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:
    import numpy as np

    layer = int(args.layers.split(",")[0])
    if layer != -1:
        emit_error_and_exit(
            "InvalidInput",
            "esm-c image supports only the final layer (--layers -1)",
            layers=args.layers,
        )
    device = pick_device(args.device)
    model = load_model(device)
    records = read_fasta(Path(args.input))
    ids = sanitize_ids([rid for rid, _ in records])
    output_dir = Path(args.output)
    warnings: list[str] = []

    pooled: dict[str, object] = {}
    artifacts: list[dict] = []
    per_residue_dir = output_dir / "per_residue"
    if args.pooling == "none":
        per_residue_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    dim = _MODEL_INFO[DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT]["embedding_dim"]
    for clean_id, (rid, seq) in zip(ids, records, strict=True):
        seq = _truncate(seq, warnings, rid)
        residue, cls_vec = _embed_one(model, seq, device)
        if args.pooling == "mean":
            pooled[clean_id] = residue.mean(axis=0)
        elif args.pooling == "cls":
            pooled[clean_id] = cls_vec
        else:  # none
            _save_npy(per_residue_dir / f"{clean_id}.npy", residue)
            artifacts.append(
                {
                    "path": f"per_residue/{clean_id}.npy",
                    "kind": "per_residue_embeddings",
                    "record_ids": [clean_id],
                    "shape": list(residue.shape),
                    "dtype": "float32",
                }
            )

    if args.pooling in ("mean", "cls"):
        np.savez(output_dir / "embeddings.npz", **pooled)
        artifacts.append(
            {
                "path": "embeddings.npz",
                "kind": "pooled_embeddings",
                "record_ids": ids,
                "shape": [len(ids), dim],
                "dtype": "float32",
            }
        )
    _write_capability_result(output_dir, "embed", records, artifacts, warnings, args)


def cmd_likelihood(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:
    device = pick_device(args.device)
    model = load_model(device)
    batch_size = args.batch_size or DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE
    records = read_fasta(Path(args.input))
    ids = sanitize_ids([rid for rid, _ in records])
    output_dir = Path(args.output)
    warnings: list[str] = []

    rows = ["record_id,seq_len,log_likelihood,mean_log_likelihood,perplexity"]
    for clean_id, (rid, seq) in zip(ids, records, strict=True):
        seq = _truncate(seq, warnings, rid)
        pll = _pseudo_log_likelihood(model, seq, batch_size, device)
        mean = pll / max(len(seq), 1)
        rows.append(f"{clean_id},{len(seq)},{pll:.6f},{mean:.6f},{perplexity_from_mean(mean):.6f}")
    (output_dir / "likelihoods.csv").write_text("\n".join(rows) + "\n")

    artifacts = [{"path": "likelihoods.csv", "kind": "likelihoods_csv", "record_ids": ids}]
    _write_capability_result(output_dir, "likelihood", records, artifacts, warnings, args)


def cmd_score(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:
    """Score variants (masked-marginal or wt-marginal) from a CSV input."""
    import csv as csv_module

    device = pick_device(args.device)
    model = load_model(device)
    batch_size = args.batch_size or DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE
    with Path(args.input).open(newline="") as handle:
        rows = list(csv_module.DictReader(handle))
    warnings: list[str] = []
    groups: dict[str, list[dict]] = {}
    for row in rows:
        groups.setdefault(row["wt_sequence"], []).append(row)

    out_rows: list[list[object]] = []
    for wt_seq, group in groups.items():
        seq = _truncate(wt_seq, warnings, "<wt>")
        positions = _valid_positions(group, seq)
        if args.method == "wt-marginal":
            logp = _wt_position_logprobs(model, seq, positions, device)
        else:
            logp = _masked_position_logprobs(model, seq, positions, batch_size, device)
        for row in group:
            score, n_mut, err = _score_variant(row["mutant"], seq, logp, model.tokenizer)
            if err is not None:
                warnings.append(f"{row['variant_id']}: {err}")
            score_str = "" if score is None else f"{score:.6f}"
            out_rows.append([row["variant_id"], row["mutant"], n_mut, score_str])

    output_dir = Path(args.output)
    with (output_dir / "scores.csv").open("w", newline="") as handle:
        writer = csv_module.writer(handle)
        writer.writerow(["variant_id", "mutant", "n_mutations", "score"])
        writer.writerows(out_rows)
    artifacts = [
        {
            "path": "scores.csv",
            "kind": "variant_scores_csv",
            "record_ids": [r["variant_id"] for r in rows],
        }
    ]
    write_result(
        output_dir,
        {
            "contract_version": CONTRACT_VERSION,
            "capability": "score",
            "model_name": DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT,
            "n_input_records": len(rows),
            "n_output_records": len(rows),
            "artifacts": artifacts,
            "warnings": warnings,
            "params": {"method": args.method, "device": args.device or "auto"},
        },
    )


def cmd_prefetch(_args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:
    """Bake weights into the image at build time (populate the HF cache)."""
    import torch
    from esm.models.esmc import ESMC

    ESMC.from_pretrained(
        DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT,
        device=torch.device("cpu"),
    )
    print(f"prefetched {DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT}")


# --- shared result writer + arg parsing ------------------------------------


def _save_npy(path: Path, array) -> None:  # noqa: ANN001
    import numpy as np

    np.save(path, array.astype(np.float32))


def _write_capability_result(
    output_dir: Path,
    capability: str,
    records: list,
    artifacts: list[dict],
    warnings: list[str],
    args: argparse.Namespace,
) -> None:
    params = {"device": args.device or "auto"}
    if capability == "embed":
        params |= {"pooling": args.pooling, "layers": args.layers}
    elif capability == "likelihood":
        params |= {"likelihood_method": "masked_marginal"}
    write_result(
        output_dir,
        {
            "contract_version": CONTRACT_VERSION,
            "capability": capability,
            "model_name": DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT,
            "n_input_records": len(records),
            "n_output_records": len(records),
            "artifacts": artifacts,
            "warnings": warnings,
            "params": params,
        },
    )


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="esm-c", description="ESM-C protlms contract entrypoint.")
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)

    sub.add_parser("manifest").set_defaults(func=cmd_manifest)

    embed = sub.add_parser("embed")
    embed.add_argument("--input", required=True)
    embed.add_argument("--output", required=True)
    embed.add_argument("--pooling", default="mean", choices=["mean", "cls", "none"])
    embed.add_argument("--layers", default="-1")
    embed.add_argument("--batch-size", type=int, default=None, dest="batch_size")
    embed.add_argument("--device", default=None, choices=["cpu", "cuda", "auto"])
    embed.set_defaults(func=cmd_embed)

    likelihood = sub.add_parser("likelihood")
    likelihood.add_argument("--input", required=True)
    likelihood.add_argument("--output", required=True)
    likelihood.add_argument("--batch-size", type=int, default=None, dest="batch_size")
    likelihood.add_argument("--device", default=None, choices=["cpu", "cuda", "auto"])
    likelihood.set_defaults(func=cmd_likelihood)

    score = sub.add_parser("score")
    score.add_argument("--input", required=True)
    score.add_argument("--output", required=True)
    score.add_argument(
        "--method", default="masked-marginal", choices=["masked-marginal", "wt-marginal"]
    )
    score.add_argument("--batch-size", type=int, default=None, dest="batch_size")
    score.add_argument("--device", default=None, choices=["cpu", "cuda", "auto"])
    score.set_defaults(func=cmd_score)

    sub.add_parser("_prefetch").set_defaults(func=cmd_prefetch)
    return parser


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> None:
    args = build_parser().parse_args(argv)
    try:
        args.func(args)
    except SystemExit:
        raise
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - top-level: report as a structured error
        emit_error_and_exit("InternalError", str(exc), exception=type(exc).__name__)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

content.text:
I've explored the codebase and have a clear picture of the pattern. Here's what's relevant for this work:

- **`containers/esm2/`** is the canonical encoder reference: HuggingFace `AutoModelForMaskedLM`, masked-marginal pseudo-log-likelihood, and `embed`/`likelihood`/`score` capabilities. Checkpoint selected via a build arg and baked into the image.
- **`containers/esm-c/`** shows the variation where the manifest is built from a hardcoded `_MODEL_INFO` table (load-free) rather than from an HF config, and where only the final layer is supported.
- The **contract** (`docs/CONTRACT.md` v0.3) defines the subcommands; encoders implement `manifest`/`embed`/`likelihood`/`score`. Adding a model = new `containers/<family>/` + a `models.yaml` entry, no client changes.

I've kicked off two background research agents to nail down the technical facts I don't want to guess at:
- **ProtBERT** — confirming `Rostlab/prot_bert(_bfd)` ids, dims/layers, and the space-separated + `U/Z/O/B→X` tokenization quirk (the main thing that differs from ESM2).
- **Profluent-E1** — the bigger unknown: encoder vs. decoder, HF availability, the single-sequence inference API, and dependencies.

I'll wait for those to report before asking clarifying questions, since the answers shape what I need to ask. Back shortly with the first question.

<result>I have everything I need. The model uses standard BERT special tokens and is a vanilla BERT architecture, so it works with any modern `transformers` version that supports BERT (which is essentially all of them). I have confirmed all six sections. Let me compile the findings.

---

# ProtBERT — Technical Facts for Toolkit Integration

Researched from the ProtTrans paper, the agemagician/ProtTrans GitHub README, and the Rostlab HuggingFace model cards (plus the raw `config.json`, `vocab.txt`, and `special_tokens_map.json` on HuggingFace, which are the authoritative source for exact dimensions).

## 1. ARCHITECTURE

**Confirmed.** ProtBERT is a BERT-style bidirectional encoder trained with masked language modeling (MLM). Each protein sequence is treated as a complete document; **no next-sentence-prediction**; 15% of residues masked (80% `[MASK]`, 10% random, 10% unchanged) — standard BERT MLM.

- Model class: **`BertForMaskedLM`** — this is the value of the `architectures` field in `config.json`.
- Loadable via HuggingFace `transformers`. The model cards show `BertForMaskedLM`/`BertModel`/`BertTokenizer` explicitly. Because `config.json` declares `architectures: ["BertForMaskedLM"]` and it is a standard BERT, the `Auto*` classes (`AutoModelForMaskedLM`, `AutoTokenizer`, `AutoConfig`, `AutoModel`) all resolve correctly. (Note: the cards' code uses the explicit `Bert*` classes; `Auto*` is equivalent but not literally quoted in the card.)
- Sources: https://huggingface.co/Rostlab/prot_bert , https://huggingface.co/Rostlab/prot_bert/raw/main/config.json , https://github.com/agemagician/ProtTrans

## 2. CHECKPOINTS

Exact HuggingFace repo ids (both confirmed in the ProtTrans README "Models Availability" table):
- **`Rostlab/prot_bert`** — trained on **UniRef100** (~217M sequences)
- **`Rostlab/prot_bert_bfd`** — trained on **BFD** (Big Fantastic Database)

Architecture is identical for both (same `config.json` dimensions):

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| hidden_size (embedding dim) | **1024** |
| num_hidden_layers | **30** |
| num_attention_heads | **16** |
| intermediate_size | **4096** |
| vocab_size (config) | **30** |
| max_position_embeddings | **40000** |
| total parameters | **~420M** |

Notes:
- **Params: ~420M** (from ProtTrans/Rostlab; consistent with the 1.68 GB fp32 weight file). The arXiv abstract page did not list the per-model count — the 420M figure comes from the model documentation/ProtTrans materials, not the abstract.
- **Vocab size nuance:** `config.json` says `vocab_size: 30`, but the model card / training docs describe an *effective* vocabulary of **21** (5 special tokens + 20 standard amino acids actively used). The `vocab.txt` actually contains 5 special tokens + 25 letters (the 20 standard AAs plus X, U, B, Z, O) = 30 entries. Use **30** for tensor shapes (the logits dimension), and be aware "21" in prose refers to the actively-trained tokens.
- **max_position_embeddings is 40000**, which is unusually large. The model was actually *trained* on sequences of length **512** (300k steps) and **2048** (100k steps). So while the position-embedding table technically allows up to 40000, practical/effective max length is ~512–2048; quality degrades well before 40000.

Other variants "worth including": the same paper ships **ProtT5** (`Rostlab/prot_t5_xl_uniref50`, `Rostlab/prot_t5_xl_bfd`), **ProtAlbert** (`Rostlab/prot_albert`), **ProtElectra**, and **ProtXLNet** — but those are different architectures. A community distilled BERT variant **`yarongef/DistilProtBert`** exists. For a "ProtBERT" entry specifically, the two canonical checkpoints are `Rostlab/prot_bert` and `Rostlab/prot_bert_bfd`.

- Sources: https://huggingface.co/Rostlab/prot_bert/raw/main/config.json , https://huggingface.co/Rostlab/prot_bert_bfd/raw/main/config.json , https://github.com/agemagician/ProtTrans

## 3. TOKENIZATION (critical)

**Confirmed on all points.**

- **Space-separated amino acids: YES.** Sequences are "uppercased and tokenized using a single space." The fill-mask example input is literally space-separated: `'D L I P T S S K L V V [MASK] D T S L Q V K K A F F A L V T'`. You must join residues with spaces (e.g. `" ".join(sequence)`) before tokenizing, otherwise the tokenizer treats the whole string as one/unknown token.
- **Rare AA mapping U, Z, O, B → X via regex: YES.** Exact line from the card:
  ```python
  sequence_Example = re.sub(r"[UZOB]", "X", sequence_Example)
  ```
- **Special tokens:** `[CLS]`, `[SEP]` added in standard BERT layout: `[CLS] seqA [SEP]` (or `[CLS] seqA [SEP] seqB [SEP]` for pairs). Full special-token set (from `special_tokens_map.json`): `pad_token=[PAD]`, `unk_token=[UNK]`, `cls_token=[CLS]`, `sep_token=[SEP]`, `mask_token=[MASK]`.
- **Tokenizer init:** `BertTokenizer.from_pretrained("Rostlab/prot_bert", do_lower_case=False)` — `do_lower_case=False` is required (amino acids are uppercase).

Exact feature-extraction snippet from the model card:
```python
from transformers import BertModel, BertTokenizer
import re
tokenizer = BertTokenizer.from_pretrained("Rostlab/prot_bert", do_lower_case=False)
model = BertModel.from_pretrained("Rostlab/prot_bert")
sequence_Example = "A E T C Z A O"
sequence_Example = re.sub(r"[UZOB]", "X", sequence_Example)
encoded_input = tokenizer(sequence_Example, return_tensors='pt')
output = model(**encoded_input)
```
- **Max sequence length / max_position_embeddings: 40000** in config; effectively trained at 512 / 2048 (see §2).
- Sources: https://huggingface.co/Rostlab/prot_bert , https://huggingface.co/Rostlab/prot_bert/raw/main/special_tokens_map.json , https://github.com/agemagician/ProtTrans

## 4. CAPABILITIES

- **(a) Per-residue embeddings via `output_hidden_states`: YES.** Standard BERT — call `model(**inputs, output_hidden_states=True)` or use `last_hidden_state` from `BertModel`, shape `(B, L, 1024)`. Strip the `[CLS]`/`[SEP]` positions to get per-residue vectors.
- **(b) Pooled embeddings (mean / CLS): YES.** Mean-pool `last_hidden_state` over real residue positions, or take the `[CLS]` token (index 0). (Note: BERT's `pooler_output` exists but is the tanh-projected CLS; for proteins mean-pooling is the common choice.)
- **(c) Per-position amino-acid logits for masked-marginal pseudo-log-likelihood: YES.** `BertForMaskedLM` outputs logits of shape `(B, L, 30)` over the vocab, which is exactly what masked-marginal / pseudo-perplexity scoring needs.
- **(d) Variant effect scores: YES, derivable** (not a built-in head). Compute via masked-marginal scoring from the MLM logits (log-ratio of mutant vs. wild-type residue probability at the masked position) — the standard ESM-style approach. ProtBERT provides the logits; the scoring logic is yours to implement.
- **Mask token for masked-marginal scoring: YES.** `mask_token = "[MASK]"`. A `fill-mask` pipeline is demonstrated in the card, confirming the MLM head and mask token work out of the box.
- Sources: https://huggingface.co/Rostlab/prot_bert , https://huggingface.co/Rostlab/prot_bert/raw/main/special_tokens_map.json

## 5. DEPENDENCIES

- **transformers / torch versions:** Neither model card nor the ProtTrans README pins exact versions — **not explicitly specified in the sources** (stated rather than guessed). However, ProtBERT is a *vanilla* `BertForMaskedLM` with no custom modeling code, so it works with effectively any modern `transformers` (4.x) on `torch` 1.x/2.x. For a reproducible Docker pin, a safe known-good combination is `transformers>=4.30,<5` with `torch>=2.0` (these support standard BERT without issue); validate against your chosen image rather than treating this as a card-sourced requirement.
- **CPU: YES.** Runs fine on CPU. Unlike the ProtT5 models, there's no fp16 caveat for BERT inference; keep it in fp32 on CPU (`model.to(torch.float32)`). It is a 420M-param model, so CPU inference on long sequences is slow but functional.
- **Size on disk:** `pytorch_model.bin` is **~1.68 GB** (fp32). No `safetensors`/`tf`/`flax` files in the main repo — only the PyTorch `.bin`. Budget ~1.7 GB per checkpoint for baking into an image (so ~3.4 GB if you bake both `prot_bert` and `prot_bert_bfd`).
- Sources: https://huggingface.co/Rostlab/prot_bert/tree/main , https://huggingface.co/Rostlab/prot_bert

## 6. LICENSE

- **Academic Free License v3.0 (AFL-3.0)** — stated in the ProtTrans GitHub README: "The ProtTrans pretrained models are released under the terms of the Academic Free License v3.0." This is a permissive license (allows commercial use, modification, distribution with attribution).
- **Gating / access restrictions: NONE found.** No gated-access flag or click-through agreement on the `Rostlab/prot_bert` or `Rostlab/prot_bert_bfd` HuggingFace pages — they download without authentication.
- Sources: https://github.com/agemagician/ProtTrans , https://huggingface.co/Rostlab/prot_bert

---

## Quick integration summary
ProtBERT = standard `BertForMaskedLM`, 30 layers / hidden 1024 / 16 heads / ~420M params, vocab dim 30. Two checkpoints: `Rostlab/prot_bert` (UniRef100) and `Rostlab/prot_bert_bfd` (BFD). **The two must-not-miss preprocessing steps:** (1) `re.sub(r"[UZOB]", "X", seq)`, then (2) space-join residues before tokenizing with `do_lower_case=False`. Supports per-residue + pooled embeddings, MLM logits for pseudo-log-likelihood/variant scoring, and `[MASK]`-based masked-marginal scoring. CPU-capable, ~1.68 GB per checkpoint, AFL-3.0, ungated.

**One open item to verify yourself:** exact transformers/torch version pins are not documented by the source — choose and test a pin for the Docker image rather than relying on a card-stated version.</result>
